key_insight: A finite element model was used to iteratively refine the WST altitude structure so it meets stiffness, stress, weight, and dynamic-resonance requirements.
engineering_relevance: The study provides design criteria and an optimized steel structural configuration for a large telescope support structure where weight and vibration directly affect optical performance.
method: Built a detailed FE model of beams and plates with representative boundary conditions.
Evaluated structural weight, deformations, stresses, and frequency response.
Used preliminary cross-sections and shell thickness results to guide mechanical optimization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the FE model was used to estimate deformation, stress, and frequency response and to identify local and global resonance modes for design refinement.
watch_item: The conclusions depend on modeled boundary conditions and preliminary geometry assumptions, so validation against physical testing or higher-fidelity operational data remains important.
paper_title: WST, the Wide-field Spectroscopic Telescope: Telescope structure FE analyses
